asset_type: TaraHome technical blog cover for a non-product Matter binding setup article
prompt:
Use case: photorealistic-natural
Asset type: TaraHome technical blog cover, landscape composition designed to survive both a 16:10 article crop and a centered 16:9 article-card crop
Primary request: create a clean editorial residential photograph about a physical Matter wall switch controlling real lamps directly even while the home automation computer is unavailable
Scene/backdrop: a warm, lived-in residential living room with off-white plaster walls, natural oak console, linen curtain, and two ordinary lamps casting warm light
Subject: a homeowner's hand pressing a simple unbranded matte-white wall paddle switch in the foreground; in the middle distance, a table lamp and floor lamp are clearly illuminated; a small generic home automation mini-computer sits unobtrusively on the oak console with its power cable visibly disconnected beside it, but the lamps remain on
Style/medium: high-end natural residential editorial photography, physically believable household scene, realistic proportions and materials, not a staged product catalog and not a conceptual tech illustration
Composition/framing: wide landscape, slight three-quarter room view, switch and hand on one side and illuminated lamps across the room; keep the switch, hand, lamps, mini-computer, and disconnected cable within the central 80 percent so centered 16:10 and 16:9 crops retain the story; no important object cut off
Lighting/mood: soft late-afternoon window light mixed with warm lamp light, calm and reassuring
Color palette: cream, warm oak, linen, matte white, with one restrained dark-green accent in a cushion or ceramic object
Materials/textures: natural oak grain, brushed wall paint, linen weave, matte plastic, ceramic lamp base, believable cable
Constraints: no readable screens, no logos, no trademarks, no brand names, no invented futuristic hardware, no embedded headline or any text, no watermark
Avoid: title poster, charts, graphs, diagrams, arrows, decorative routing/network/path lines, glowing connection trails, translucent panels, inset frames, floating UI cards, progress bars, badges, labels, fake app interfaces, neon, generic AI-looking smart-home diagram, repeated beige device diagram, showroom-perfect product grid
